config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu harus tetap menjadi batas utama, karena kalau kepentingan masyarakat selalu diutamakan tanpa kontrol, mudah sekali dipakai untuk membenarkan penindasan atas nama mayoritas. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi seharusnya dicapai dengan menghormati hak dasar tiap orang, bukan mengorbankannya. Dalam pandangan saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang dilindungi martabat dan kebebasannya.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not believe society should take priority over the individual as a blanket rule, because in the United States we have seen how quickly “the common good” can be used to excuse abuse of minorities or unpopular people. That said, individual rights are not absolute in every case, since public health, safety, and basic civic order sometimes require limits. The real standard should be whether the restriction is narrowly justified and protects others without treating people as disposable. If society always comes first, the individual becomes too easy to silence.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap tidak setuju bahwa kepentingan masyarakat harus didahulukan atas hak individu sebagai prinsip umum. Dalam pengalaman saya melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan bersama” terlalu sering dipakai untuk menekan suara minoritas, membungkam kritik, atau memaksa orang patuh tanpa akuntabilitas yang jelas. Memang ada situasi tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan, tetapi pembatasannya harus sangat terbatas, transparan, dan tidak boleh menginjak hak dasar. Kalau hak individu tidak dijaga, “kepentingan masyarakat” mudah berubah jadi kedok untuk kekuasaan mayoritas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general rule, because in the United States we have too much history of “society first” being used to justify discrimination, censorship, and abuses against unpopular groups. At the same time, I do not treat individual rights as unlimited, since things like public health, emergency response, and basic safety sometimes require real constraints. What matters to me is whether the limit is narrow, accountable, and actually necessary rather than just convenient for those in power. If society always takes priority, the individual loses the protection that makes a free society worth having.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu sebagai prinsip umum, karena di Indonesia alasan “demi masyarakat” terlalu mudah dipakai untuk menekan minoritas, kritik, atau orang yang tidak sejalan dengan arus mayoritas. Memang ada keadaan tertentu seperti kesehatan publik atau keamanan yang bisa membenarkan pembatasan, tetapi pembatasannya harus ketat, sementara, dan bisa diuji akuntabilitasnya. Kalau hak individu dikalahkan terus-menerus atas nama kepentingan bersama, yang terjadi bukan masyarakat yang kuat, melainkan kekuasaan yang makin leluasa. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat justru dibangun dari individu yang hak dasarnya dilindungi, bukan dikorbankan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not accept the statement as a general principle, because in the United States I have seen how easily “society first” turns into excuses for discrimination, censorship, and trampling unpopular people. At the same time, I do not believe individual rights are absolute in every situation, since public health, safety, and emergency conditions can justify narrow limits when they are truly necessary. What I reject is the idea that society should automatically outrank the individual, because that gives too much power to majorities and institutions. A free society depends on protecting individual rights first, while allowing only carefully limited exceptions.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
